ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Di saha ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa guui ti tuh’ o soli yɛl’ o, “N suhir’ a, tim ma a koyuli ŋɔ ni kom biɛla ka n nyu.”

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: A takɔb’ zuɣu ka a yɛn dira, ka jɛmd’ a tizo maa. Amaa a yi zaɣis’ amaŋ’ zuɣu, a ni yi o sulinsi ni.”
translated: The elder from the north did the work and carried his own burden. But Amaa went to Zaɣis’ place in the north, and he went to resolve it.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: O daa yɛl’ o tumtumdiba kpɛma, “M biɛli Iso yi ti tuhi ya soli bɔhi ya, ‘Ŋuni n-lee sun ya? Ka ya polo ka yi lee chana? Ka ŋuni n-lee su biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn be yi tooni ŋɔ?’
translated: He spoke in a parable, "I know the Creator who made you and gave you life. 'Whose face did you see? And your body, did you see its inside? And who did you see, the one who digs the grave or the one who will take you tomorrow?'"

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Ka di daa niŋ zaawuni, di nyaaŋa ka biɛɣu neei; ka di niŋ dabisili din pah’ anu.
translated: And you do with Zaawuni, you follow and take it; and with Dabisili, you carry this.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Ka bihi daa ti var’ o pul’ ni ka o yɛli, “Di yi yɛn nyɛ ŋ-ŋɔ n ni be?” Ka o daa chaŋ ni o ti bɔhi Yawɛ nya.
translated: And when he went to speak to Pul, he said, "What is your name?" And he answered and called the name Yaw.
